LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SEARIVER MARTIME INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE NEGLIGENCE PER SE CLAIM IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS NOT MET HIS INITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION OF EVIDENCE. DEFENDANT DOES NOT ADDRESS ALL REGULATORY VIOLATIONS IDENTIFIED IN PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSE TO FORM INTERROGATORY NO. 14.1 (I.E. 33 CFR 96.230 AND 96.240). THE EXISTENCE OF ANY SINGLE ASSERTED REGULATORY VIOLATION WOULD RAISE AN ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT FOR TRIAL. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: RICHARD A. KRAMER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN CSR6185.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF RAYMOND SARNO'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED IN PART, AND DENIED IN PART. DEFENDANT SHALL PRODUCE ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS THAT DO NOT CONTAIN MR. QUINONES' THOUGHTS, IMPRESSIONS, OBSERVATIONS AND/OR THOUGHTS ON THE SUBJECT INCIDENT AS THEY ARE ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT. DEFENDANT SHALL PRODUCE A PRIVILEGE LOG FOR ALL WITHHELD DOCUMENTS. SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. WITH RESPECT TO REQUEST NO. 32, DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO PRODUCE THE TABLE OF CONTENTS AND THE PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER FURTHER TO DETERMINE WHICH SECTIONS WILL BE PRODUCED. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SEARIVER MARITIME INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO AUGUST 12, 2013. JUDGE: PAUL J. ALVARADO, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN CSR6185.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SEARIVER MARITIME INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE JONES ACT AND UNSEAWORTHINESS CLAIMS ARE DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO MEET ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION OF EVIDENCE TO SHOW THAT THERE IS NO ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO I) WHETHER PLAINTIFF SUFFERED PHYSICAL INJURY AND II) IF NOT, WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS IN THE "ZONE OF DANGER" FOR PURPOSES OF THE APPLICABLE TEST SET FORTH IN THE PARTIES' PAPERS. (SEE CONSOLIDATED RAIL CORP. V. GOTTSHALL (1994) 512 U.S. 532, 556.) IN REPLY, DEFENDANT REFERS TO MATERIAL FACTS AND EVIDENCE THAT WERE NOT ADDRESSED IN ITS MOVING SEPARATE STATEMENT AND HAS THUS DEMONSTRATED THAT THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: RICHARD A. KRAMER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN CSR6185.

Defendant Seariver Martime Inc'S Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JOHN HILLSMAN, ESQ., 415.421.9292, FOR PLAINTIFF RAYMOND SARNO.
DAVID RUSSO, ESQ., 415.543.5300, FOR DEFENDANT SEARIVER MARITIME INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendant Seariver Martime Inc'S Motion For Summary Adjudication of the request for punitive damages pertaining to the Jones Act and unseaworthiness claims are denied. Defendant cannot seek piecemeal adjudication of punitive damages. (CCP 437c(f); Catalano v. Sup.Ct. (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 91, 97.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Richard A. Kramer, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR6185.

Defendant Seariver Martime Inc Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JOHN HILLSMAN, ESQ., 415.421.9292, FOR PLAINTIFF RAYMOND SARNO.
DAVID RUSSO, ESQ., 415.543.5300, FOR DEFENDANT SEARIVER MARITIME INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendant Seariver Martime Inc.'s Motion For Summary Adjudication of the negligence per se claim is denied. Defendant has not met his initial burden of production of evidence. Defendant does not address all regulatory violations identified in Plaintiff's response to Form Interrogatory no. 14.1 (i.e. 33 CFR 96.230 and 96.240). The existence of any single asserted regulatory violation would raise an issue of material fact for trial. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Richard A. Kramer, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR6185.

Defendant Seariver Maritime Inc'S Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JOHN HILLSMAN, ESQ., 415.421.9292, FOR PLAINTIFF RAYMOND SARNO.
DAVID RUSSO, ESQ., 415.543.5300, FOR DEFENDANT SEARIVER MARITIME INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendant Seariver Maritime Inc'S Motion For Summary Adjudication of the Jones Act and unseaworthiness claims are denied. Defendant has failed to meet its burden of production of evidence to show that there is no issue of material fact as to i) whether plaintiff suffered physical injury and ii) if not, whether plaintiff was in the "zone of danger" for purposes of the applicable test set forth in the parties' papers. (See Consolidated Rail Corp. v. Gottshall (1994) 512 U.S. 532, 556.) In reply, Defendant refers to material facts and evidence that were not addressed in its moving separate statement and has thus demonstrated that there are triable issues of fact. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Richard A. Kramer, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR6185.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S First Request For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Jan-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. LIA T. MARKS OF THE LAW OFFICES OF LYLE C. CAVIN JR., TEL. NO.
(510) 444-2501 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DAVID E. RUSSO OF STERLING & CLACK, TEL. NO. 543-5300 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
DENISE A. FORD OF LEGALINK, TEL. NO. 357-4300, COURT REPORTER.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF RAYMOND SARNO's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S First Request For Production Of Documents is Granted in part, and denied in part. Defendant shall produce all responsive documents that do not contain Mr. Quinones' thoughts, impressions, observations and/or thoughts on the subject incident as they are attorney work product. Defendant shall produce a privilege log for all withheld documents. Sanctions are denied. With respect to Request No. 32, defendant is ordered to produce the table of contents and the parties to meet and confer further to determine which sections will be produced. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Denise Ford, CSR # 7525
=(302/MJM)
